description: §19.5 `?` propagation (RT): inner('missing') fails LoadError.NotFound; outer calls inner(id)? which propagates the error THROUGH outer's own `!` boundary; the call-site `!{}` handler on outer('missing') then catches the propagated .NotFound(mid) and lifts @phase = Phase.Missing(mid). Proves `?` carried the error across the intermediate failable frame to the caller's handler. LoadError is the error type of BOTH inner and outer, so §19.5.3 compatibility holds (no E-TYPE-001).
rationale: §19.5.2: `?` on a failable call returns the error variant from the enclosing `!` function. So outer('missing') resolves to LoadError.NotFound('missing'), which the call-site `!{}` catches -> @phase lands Phase.Missing with the propagated payload. The whole E-ERROR-* family stays silent (handled call, §19.6.6; `?` in a `!` fn is legal so no E-ERROR-003/004).
